questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Prime Minister assured the house that before signing the WTO agreement, every aspects will be discussed thoroughly.
options: before signing WTO agreement, every aspects would be discussed thoroughly.
before signing the WTO agreement, every aspects will be thoroughly discussed.
before being signed, the WTO agreement will be discussed thoroughly.
before signing the WTO agreement, every aspect would be discussed thoroughly.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of number and tense. Adjective ‘every’ should be followed by singular noun (aspect), while the verb ‘will’ should be ‘would’ to agree with the tense of the verb of principal clause (assured).

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Within three years, he demonstrated a dramatic improved business performance.
options: the dramatic improved
the dramatically improved
a dramatically improved
a dramatic improvement
questions.solution:
In the sentence the word before the phrase 'improved business performance' must be an adverb as it has to add information to the adjective 'improved'.

The underlined part 'a dramatic improved' hence must be replaced with 'a dramatically improved' to make it a grammatically correct sentence.

Hence, the option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Your defence that you found the purse lying on the floor cannot be trust.
options: cannot be trusted
can hardly be trust
can never be trust
could not be trust
questions.solution:
The sentence is in passive voice and hence past particple (third form) of the verb 'trust' that is 'trusted' will be used to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
If in case you want to resign within one month, you will have to pay Rs 10,000.
options: If the case
If the case of
If at all in case
In case
questions.solution:
The idiom 'in case' means 'if it happens' and putting another 'if' before the idiom doesn't make sense. 

The underlined part 'If in case' hence must be replaced with 'In case' to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Had he been presented there, he would have put an end to the happenings.
options: If he has been presented
If he had been present
Had he presented
If he had present
questions.solution:
In this sentence, which is a type 3 conditional sentence, adding 'ed' to the adjective 'present' is erroneous. 

This sentence can be written in two ways:
"Had he been present there, he would have put an end to the happenings." or "If he had been present there, he would have put an end the happenings." 

The option B, therefore, will make a right choice. 

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
A preventive programme for the break out of epidemic in quake-hit areas must be introduced.
options: A preventive programme before the break out of
A preventive programme for the breaking out of
A preventive programme for the out break of
A preventive programme before the out break of
questions.solution:
Corrects the misuse of phrasal verb for noun. ‘Breakout’ is a phrasal verb meaning ‘to start or appear suddenly’ while ‘out break’ is a noun meaning ‘sudden appearance’. A preposition or an article is used before a noun, not a verb. Use preposition ‘before’ in place of ‘for’ because a preventive measure is taken before the appearance of epidemic.

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
My brother’s face resembles to me and people often get confused when they see him.
options: My brother’s face resemble for me
My brother’s face resembles me
Face of my brother resembles to me
My brother’s face resemble to me
questions.solution:
Delete preposition ‘to’ after resemble’.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
The scenery around the hill station of Himachal Pradesh is quite picturesque and enjoyed.
options: quite picturesque and enjoyable
quite picturesque and enjoyed
quietly picturesque and enjoyed
quietly picturesque and enjoyable
questions.solution:
The word 'enjoyed' must be replaced with the adjective 'enjoyable' to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

Hence, the optioin A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Everyone agrees that no one besides Tendulkar could have won the match for India.
options: no one except Tendulkar could win the match of India.
no one besides Tendulkar could have won the matches of India.
no one except Tendulkar could won the match for India.
no one but Tendulkar could have won the match for India.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of preposition. ‘But’ excludes while ‘besides’ includes. ‘But’ is used after negative no body, and none meaning ‘except’. While ‘besides’ means ‘in addition to’.

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Since the military take over in Pakistan, the family members of deposed the Prime Minister are confined within their houses.
options: are confined to their houses.
are confined in their houses.
is confined within their houses.
is confined to his houses.
questions.solution:
‘Confined’ is followed by preposition ‘to’.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
We had hardly started watching the movie than the fire brokeout.
options: to watch the movie when the fire brokeout.
watching the movie when the fire brokeout.
watching the movie then the fire brokeout.
to watch the movie then the fire brokeout.
questions.solution:
‘hardly’ is followed by ‘when’.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
 A series of seminars, to evolve consensus on CTBT were organized by the government and various organizations.
options: evolving consensus for CTBT was organised.
to evolve consensus about CTBT were organised.
evolving consensus about CTBT were organised.
to evolve consensus on CTBT was organised.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of subject verb agreement. Because the subject of the verb (was) is ‘A series of seminars’, conveys a singular meaning or one idea (number of events of similar kind).

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
For a whole, the alternative to big dams suggested by experts, appear to be superior and more feasible than any other alternative that one can think of.
options: appear to be superior to and more feasible
appear to be more superior and more feasible
appears to be more superior and feasible
appears to be superior to and more feasible
questions.solution:
‘The alternative’ is the subject for the verb 'appear' and since the subject is singular in form the verb must be 'appears' instead of 'appear'.

Besides, ‘superior’ or ‘inferior’ has the force of comparative degree and is followed by ‘to’ and not ‘than’. 
When two adjectives require different prepositions, appropriate prepositions should be used with both adjectives. e.g.,
 
Ex. He is senior and older than I. (Use ‘to’ after ‘senior’)
Ex. His dress is different and cheaper than mine. (Use ‘from’ after ‘different’)
Ex. She is stronger and younger than her sister. (Correct)

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Even before the villagers could be shifted to a safer place, the river had over flown its bank.
options: the river had overflowed its bank.
the river had flown over its bank.
the river overflow its bank.
the river over flowed its bank.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error in diction, ‘flown’ is past participle form of ‘fly’ while ‘flowed’ is past participle form of ‘flow’.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
The computer I have bought today is far more superior to the one I had bought last year.
options: far superior to the one that had been bought last year.
far superior to the one I had bought last year.
far superior than that of one I bought last year.
far more superior than that of one I had bought last year.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of double comparative. Because ‘superior’ in itself has the force of comparative degree, so use of ‘more’ before it, is superfluous.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Attached to this application is an affidavit form and three sheets of blank paper.
options: are an affidavit form and three sheet
are an affidavit form and three sheets
is an affidavit form and three sheet
is a affidavit form and three sheet
questions.solution:
Corrects the subject verb agreement. The subject of the verb ‘are’ is an affidavit form and three sheets (plural) not ‘the application’.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
​ A good boss does not waste his valuable time finding faults with his subordinates.
options: finding fault with his subordinates.
to find faults with his subordinates.
to find faults in his subordinates.
finding faults of his subordinates.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of idiomatic use. ‘Find fault with’ is an idiom meaning ‘to look for mistakes or to complain about something’.

Hence, the optiion A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Her supporters rallied for her defence when the government brought the bill seeking to bar any person of foreign origin from holding the high offices.
options: Her supporters rallied of her defence
Her supporters rallied in her defence
Her supporters rallied up in her defence
Her supporters rallied to her defence
questions.solution:
When people rally to something or when something rallies them, they unite to support it.

Ex. His supporters have rallied to his defence.
Ex. He rallied his own supporters for a fight.

The preposition 'in/at' is followed by the verb 'rally' only in a context of a place.

Ex. Ian Smith, a 24-year-old who works with adolescents in drug and mental health rehab, showed up at the same Trump rally in suburban Southern California.

Ex. Fistfights broke out among protesters who were rallying at the JNU, prompting the management to cancel the event.

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
The smooth passage of the Insurance Bill through the Lok Sabha is the best news which India has have in recent years.
options: is the best news that India has in recent years.
is the best news which India has had in recent years.
is the best news that India has had in recent years.
is the best news India has in recent years.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of past participle form of verb ‘have’ and conjunction ‘that’. In perfect tense after has/have/had, the transitive or main verb is used in V3 (past participle form). In the sentence, V3 form of ‘have’ is ‘had’ meaning ‘experienced’. After adjective in superlative degree, ‘that’ is used to join the clauses.

Hence, the option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
 Polygamy is practised among muslims but women organizations are demanding a law against this practice.
options: is practised by muslims but women’s organizations
is practised between muslims but women organizations
is practised among muslims but women organization
is practised by muslims but women organization
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of use of preposition. Because the custom is prevalent among muslims and it is practised by them, so use of possessive case is correct, because voices against this practice are being raised by women’s organisations or organisation of women.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
 India has the largest number of cattles of all other countries of the world.
options: cattles from all the countries of the world.
cattles of all the countries of the world.
cattle of all other countries of the world.
cattle of all the countries of the world.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of number and comparison by means of superlative degree. ‘Cattle’ is always used as plural noun, it does take ‘s’ or ‘es’ at end. While comparing by means of superlative, the latter term should include the former. So, delete the use of ‘other’ because, it excludes the former term (India).

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
The rail minister is arriving by Rajdhani Express which is due to arrive to 12 PM.
options: arriving on Rajdhani Express that is due to arrive for 12 PM.
arriving by Rajdhani Express which is due to arrive at 12 PM.
arriving by Rajdhani Express is due on 12 PM.
arriving on Rajdhani Express is due to arrive about 12 PM.
questions.solution:
Because the train is scheduled to arrive at 12 PM (‘at’ should be used to express point of time).

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
The Delhi Police and the CBI, the court have observed, not only tampered with evidence but also manipulated the circumstantial evidence too.
options: has observed, no tampered only evidence
has observed, not only tampered with evidence
has observed, not only tampered of evidence
have observed, not tampered with only evidence
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of agreement. Because the observation has been made by the court, ‘tampered with’ is a phrasal verb meaning ‘to interfere or meddle with’.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Last but not the least, I would like to thank all the workers without whose co-operation, the rally wouldn’t be a success.
options: Last but not least, I would like to thank all the workers without their co-operation
Last but not the least, I would like to thank all the worker without their co-operation
Last but not least, I would like to thank all the workers without whose co-operation
Last but not the least, I would like to thank all those workers without their co-operation
questions.solution:
Corrects the idiomatic error. ‘Last but not least’ is an idiom which means ‘last but not less important than others’.

Hence, the option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Such a consensus denotes a remarkable change of attitude for this very measure had run into trouble in the earlier stages.
options: a remarkable change of attitude as this measure was run of trouble
a remarkable change of attitude as this very measure had run into trouble
a remarkable change of attitude because this very measure had run into trouble
a remarkable change in attitude because this measure was run into very trouble
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of conjunction ‘such’. Because it is always followed by ‘as’ or ‘that’.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
He did not like me to smoking in the presence of our teacher yesterday.
options: that I smoke
my smoking
me smoking
smoking by me
questions.solution:
The underlined part 'me to smoking' must be replaced with 'my smoking' to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

As per the basic usage rules, 'like' is one of the verbs which are followed by a gerund e.g. swmming, dancing or travelling etc. 

Since the gerund is a noun, it is logical to find it preceded by a possessive pronoun (his, her, your, my, our, etc) or a noun in the possessive form (Rohan's, Mr. Sharma's, etc) 

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Expeditiously completion of the process will be appreciated by everyone involved.
options: Expeditiously complete
Expeditious complete
Expeditious completion
Expedite completion
questions.solution:
As completion is a noun, the word before it has to be an adjective adding information to it. The advrb 'Expeditiously' hence must be replaced with the adjective 'Expeditious' to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

Hence, the option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
He asked his supporters to prepare them for shouldering greater responsibilities.
options: to preparing themselves to shoulder greater responsibilities.
preparing them for shouldering greater responsibilities.
to prepare themselves for shouldering greater responsibilities.
to prepare them to shouldering greater responsibilities.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of reflective pronoun. In the above sentence, ‘them’ has come for ‘his supporters’ it means the action reflects back to the doer or subject ‘his supporters’.

Hence, the option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
His immediate task may be to keep the coalition in the state going unless he can think of ways to counter Mr. Singh’s mission now to demolish the parent party.
options: unless he can think about ways countering Mr. Singh’s mission
unless he can’t think of ways to counter mission of Mr. Singh
till he can think of ways to counter Mr. Singh’s mission
till he can think ways to counter mission of Mr. Singh
questions.solution:
'Unless’ is a conjunction of condition while ‘till’ refers to ‘time’ meaning ‘as far as the time when or before the time when’.

In the given sentence, ‘his task may be to keep the coalition going till the time when he can think of ways or finds ways to counter Mr. Singh’s mission’ (means the subject seeks time to think ways to counter Singh’s mission. It is referring to time, not condition.)

Option C hence is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Had the party dispensed of the contentious issues it would have got the majority at its own.
options: Had the party dispensed without the contentious issues
Was the party dispensed of the contentious issues
Was the party dispensed without the contentious issues
Had the party dispensed with the contentious issues
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of phrasal verb. ‘To dispense with’ means ‘to manage without or to get rid of’.

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Candidates put up by the do-gooders including me were elected with thumping majority.
options: Candidates put out by the do-gooders including me were
Candidates put up by the do-gooders including me was
Candidates put by the do-gooders including me was
Candidates put up by the do-gooders including myself were
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of reflective pronoun.

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
But it hardly can ignore the long term impact of his rebellion and the message it sends to the party workers.
options: But it can ignore hardly the long term impact of
But it can hardly ignore the long term impact of
But it cannot hardly ignore the long term impact of
But hardly it can ignore long term impact of
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of placing of adverb of degree. Adverb of degree comes before the word it modifies. In the given sentence ‘hardly’ is expected to modify ‘ignore’ to express the meaning that ‘it almost can’t ignore the long term impact of’.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Never before have the words ‘social service’ been as prostituted as it is today.
options: so prostituted as it is today.
as prostituted, it is today.
so prostituted they are today.
as prostituted as they are today.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of pronoun. Because, pronoun ‘it’ is used for plural noun ‘the words’. So instead of ‘it’, ‘they’ should be used and verb should be ‘are’.

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
For the old it is only a few benches or steps of old building to sit and brood at days gone away.
options: to sit and brood at the days gone off.
to sit and brood for the days gone off.
to sit and brood over the days gone by.
to sit and brood for the days gone by.
questions.solution:
Corrects error of preposition, definite article and phrasal verb. ‘Brood’ is followed by on/over. Days will be preceded by definite article ‘the’ because the days ‘gone by’ are talked of (or particularised).

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
 As good as a banker is concerned, the law relating to indemnities is of great importance.
options: As well as
As much as
As far as
As soon as
questions.solution:
Out of the given choices 'as far as' is the most appropriate option to choose.

As far as (idiom): to the degree that ...

Ex. As far as I know, he isn't coming to the party.

Hence, the option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
In traditional India, respect for woman is entirely contingent at their patriarchal whims.
options: is entirely contingent on her patriarchal whims.
entirely is contingent at their patriarchal whims.
is entirely contingent to her patriarchal whim.
entirely is contingent to her patriarchal whims.
questions.solution:
Corrects prepositional error and error of pronoun. ‘Her’ refers to ‘woman’ (singular). Contingent should be followed by preposition on/upon meaning dependent on/upon.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
 The academic council has requested the principal to mediate and help settle the dispute.
options: and help to settle the dispute.
and to help to settle the dispute.
and to help settle the dispute.
and helping to settle the dispute.
questions.solution:
Corrects the error in the parallel construction of infinitive. It should be as ‘to mediate and to help’.

Hence, the option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Kashmir is a complicated problem that what world community believes.
options: which world community believes.
what world community believes.
that what world community believe.
what world community believe.
questions.solution:
Delete ‘that’. Because ‘what’ is not preceded by ‘that’ as ‘what’ itself means ‘that which’.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
 Though he has been receiving all the facilities he is not entitled for.
options: but he is not entitled of.
he is not entitled of.
but he is not entitled for.
he is not entitled to.
questions.solution:
Corrects prepositional error.

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
A student was arrested for displaying an indecently art work in public.
options: indecent
unindecently
the indecently
any of indecently
questions.solution:
'Art work' is a noun phrase, before which an appropriate adjective should be used. 

The underlined part 'indecently', which is an adverb, hence must be replaced with the adjective 'indecent' to make it a grammatically correct sentence.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
The subsidiary banks of the State bank of India were established by different special statues.
options: special status
special statutes
especial states
specialised statutes
questions.solution:
Special statute
an act of the legislature which has reference to a particular person, place, or interest; a private law; - in distinction from a general law or public law.

Option B hence is the most appropriate choice here.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Being a convict of fodder scam, resignation from the post was expected from him.
options: Being convict of fodder scam, he had been expected to resign from the post.
He being a convict of fodder scam, resignation from the post was expected from him.
Being a convict of fodder scam, he expected to resign from the post.
No correction required
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of dangling modifier. Because, a participle is a verbal-adjective, and it must be attached to a proper subject. In the sentence, being a convict of fodder scam, is modifying ‘the resignation from the post’. ‘D’ corrects this error.

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
All banks whose shares are listed with stock exchanges are required to publish their unaudited quarterly results as per performance prescribed by the SEBI.
options: as per proforma
as according to pro forma
as required by pro forma
according to performance
questions.solution:
In banking, a company's pro forma balance or earnings are their expected balance or earnings. 

The term pro forma (Latin for "as a matter of form" or "for the sake of form") is most often used to describe a practice or document that is provided as a courtesy and/or satisfies minimum requirements, conforms to a norm or doctrine, tends to be performed perfunctorily and/or is considered a formality.

Option A hence is the most appropriate choice here.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Hot deserts are found near the western coasts of continents because the winds passing by the cold ocean currents blowing along these coasts are dry.
options: the winds passing over the cold ocean currents flowing along these coasts
the wind passing through the cold ocean current blowing along these coasts
the wind passes through the cold ocean current blowing along these coasts
the wind passes by the cold ocean current flowing from these coasts
questions.solution:
Corrects the error of preposition and diction. Because, ‘wind passes over the cold currents’ and wind blows, while water flows. So ‘Ocean currents flowing’ is the correct expression.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following questions, some part of the sentence is underlined. Which of the options given below the sentence should replace the part underlined to make the sentence grammatically correct? If the sentence is correct as it is given then choose option E ‘No Correction required’ as the answer.
Since an advertising campagin’s success hinges on consumer acceptance a large body of research naturally focus on how people decode advertising stimuli.
options: a large body of research naturally focuses why people decode advertising stimuli.
a large body of research naturally focuses on how people decode advertising stimuli.
a large body of research naturally lays focus on how people decode advertising stimuli.
a large body of research naturally focused on how people decode advertising stimuli.
questions.solution:
Option A is incorrect because a thing or idea is ‘focused on’ not ‘focused why’.
 
Option C is incorrect as well because a focus is not laid upon something. 
 
Option D has a tense error with the word ‘focused’. It should be ‘focuses’ to match with “hinges” which is in the present tense.
 
Clearly, option B is hence the correct answer.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
